1.A.J. EwingLHBNYM vs Elmer Rodríguez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.335 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.435 (+20 pts)14d —vs RHP —K 9.5%BB 27.0%HR 9.0%Hit 9.0%Even

A.J. Ewing LHB vs Elmer Rodríguez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 76+0+0, pitcher rows 174+0+0

Verdict

Rodríguez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while A.J. Ewing should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.335 xwOBA (+20 pts vs league, Hitter). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

2.Kyle SchwarberLHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.346 / xBA 0.222 / xSLG 0.425 (+31 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.457vs RHP 0.377K 33.9%BB 10.4%HR 8.4%Hit 18.2%Slight Hitter

Kyle Schwarber LHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 813+3157+2950, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Schwarber should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.346 xwOBA (+31 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.326 -> adj 0.314); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.314 -> final 0.346); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Schwarber is on a 14-day heater (+58 pts vs season). Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Brandon Valenzuela LHB vs Jack Flaherty RHP

Verdict

Flaherty's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Brandon Valenzuela should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.390 xwOBA (+75 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +63 pts (bb_type-adj 0.324 -> final 0.390); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

4.Colson MontgomeryLHBCWS vs Colin Rea (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.376 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.405 (+61 pts)Park +20 pts14d 0.327vs RHP 0.374K 23.1%BB 9.2%HR 7.3%Hit 24.2%Edge Hitter

Colson Montgomery LHB vs Colin Rea RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 638+1088+0, pitcher rows 729+2657+2656

Verdict

Rea's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Colson Montgomery should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.376 xwOBA (+61 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.345 -> adj 0.323); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +33 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.376); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Colin Rea is in a 14-day rough patch (+96 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

5.Kyle ManzardoLHBCLE vs Brady Singer (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.383 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.437 (+68 pts)Park +11 pts14d 0.436vs RHP 0.435K 24.7%BB 8.7%HR 7.3%Hit 22.9%Edge Hitter

Kyle Manzardo LHB vs Brady Singer RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 548+2074+709, pitcher rows 734+2850+2975

Verdict

Projection: 0.383 xwOBA (+68 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.350 -> adj 0.337); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +35 pts (bb_type-adj 0.337 -> final 0.383); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Manzardo is on a 14-day heater (+128 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

6.Shohei OhtaniLHBLAD vs Grayson Rodriguez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.369 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.466 (+54 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.307vs RHP 0.351K 28.2%BB 11.8%HR 7.3%Hit 21.3%Edge Hitter

Shohei Ohtani LHB vs Grayson Rodriguez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 716+3241+3152, pitcher rows 0+0+1879

Verdict

Projection: 0.369 xwOBA (+54 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.358 -> adj 0.336); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +39 pts (bb_type-adj 0.336 -> final 0.369); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shohei Ohtani is on a 14-day slump (-48 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

7.Hunter GoodmanRHBCOL vs Michael Soroka (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.353 / xBA 0.232 / xSLG 0.404 (+38 pts)Park +16 pts14d 0.240vs RHP 0.216K 27.9%BB 4.9%HR 7.2%Hit 21.5%Slight Hitter

Hunter Goodman RHB vs Michael Soroka RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 621+2151+828, pitcher rows 729+1572+1447

Verdict

Soroka's slurve is the projected matchup advantage, while Hunter Goodman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.353 xwOBA (+38 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.308 -> final 0.353); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Hunter Goodman is on a 14-day slump (-64 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

8.Mickey GasperLHBBOS vs Grant Holmes (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.331 / xBA 0.264 / xSLG 0.414 (+16 pts)Park -13 pts14d —vs RHP —K 17.4%BB 9.6%HR 6.9%Hit 18.9%Even

Mickey Gasper LHB vs Grant Holmes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 75+451+92, pitcher rows 656+1970+1029

Verdict

Holmes' curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Mickey Gasper should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.331 xwOBA (+16 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.333 -> final 0.331); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

9.Daulton VarshoLHBTOR vs Jack Flaherty (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.355 / xBA 0.253 / xSLG 0.405 (+40 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.305vs RHP 0.317K 27.3%BB 9.7%HR 6.8%Hit 21.4%Slight Hitter

Daulton Varsho LHB vs Jack Flaherty RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 573+1298+2054, pitcher rows 786+3028+3034

Verdict

Flaherty's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Daulton Varsho should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.355 xwOBA (+40 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.324 -> final 0.355); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 72.9 mph
swing length 7.7 ft
attack angle 11.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+7.0222+2.3on plane
Slider-7.5+16.366+8.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Knuckle Curve-9.4+18.112+8.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-6.9+17.462+10.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-6.1+6.051-0.1on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

10.Aaron JudgeRHBNYY vs Freddy Peralta (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.360 / xBA 0.235 / xSLG 0.446 (+45 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.374vs RHP 0.387K 29.9%BB 12.4%HR 6.7%Hit 18.7%Slight Hitter

Aaron Judge RHB vs Freddy Peralta RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 832+2875+3235, pitcher rows 762+3365+3151

Verdict

Peralta's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Aaron Judge should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.360 xwOBA (+45 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -40 pts (raw 0.373 -> adj 0.333); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.333 -> final 0.360); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Judge is on a 14-day slump (-55 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

11.Seiya SuzukiRHBCHC vs Erick Fedde (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.375 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.442 (+60 pts)Park +23 pts14d 0.301vs RHP 0.338K 20.2%BB 7.8%HR 6.5%Hit 25.3%Edge Hitter

Seiya Suzuki RHB vs Erick Fedde RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 543+2841+2520, pitcher rows 675+2374+2895

Verdict

Fedde's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Seiya Suzuki should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.375 xwOBA (+60 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.338 -> adj 0.328); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.328 -> final 0.375); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Seiya Suzuki is on a 14-day slump (-44 pts vs season). Erick Fedde is in a 14-day rough patch (+103 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

12.Junior CamineroRHBTB vs Eury Pérez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.323 / xBA 0.231 / xSLG 0.425 (+8 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.354vs RHP 0.316K 23.6%BB 7.0%HR 6.5%Hit 20.7%Even

Verdict

Pérez's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Junior Caminero should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.323 xwOBA (+8 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.328 -> adj 0.310); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.310 -> final 0.323); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. 13.Munetaka MurakamiLHBCWS vs Colin Rea (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.295 / xBA 0.296 / xSLG 0.455 (-20 pts)Park +19 pts14d 0.336vs RHP 0.351K 22.5%BB 11.5%HR 6.3%Hit 16.2%Even

Munetaka Murakami LHB vs Colin Rea RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 811+0+0, pitcher rows 729+2657+2656

Verdict

Rea's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Munetaka Murakami should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.295 xwOBA (-20 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.351 -> adj 0.357); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -81 pts (bb_type-adj 0.357 -> final 0.295); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Colin Rea is in a 14-day rough patch (+96 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Rodriguez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Teoscar Hernández should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.349 xwOBA (+34 pts vs league, Hitter). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

15.Eric HaaseRHBSF vs Jeffrey Springs (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.308 / xBA 0.261 / xSLG 0.463 (-7 pts)Park +15 pts14d —vs LHP —K 22.1%BB 7.8%HR 6.2%Hit 20.7%Even

Eric Haase RHB vs Jeffrey Springs LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 74+339+270, pitcher rows 785+2849+565

Verdict

Springs' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Eric Haase should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.308 xwOBA (-7 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.327 -> adj 0.342); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -49 pts (bb_type-adj 0.342 -> final 0.308); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jeffrey Springs is in a 14-day rough patch (+56 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

16.José RamírezRHBCLE vs Brady Singer (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.342 / xBA 0.275 / xSLG 0.408 (+27 pts)Park +10 pts14d 0.324vs RHP 0.284K 12.3%BB 6.3%HR 6.1%Hit 22.7%Slight Hitter

José Ramírez RHB vs Brady Singer RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 823+2816+2971, pitcher rows 734+2850+2975

Verdict

Singer's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while José Ramírez should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.342 xwOBA (+27 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.309 -> adj 0.327); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.327 -> final 0.342); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 17.Jake BurgerRHBTEX vs Peter Lambert (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.370 / xBA 0.275 / xSLG 0.482 (+55 pts)Park -12 pts14d 0.356vs RHP 0.378K 18.8%BB 4.0%HR 6.0%Hit 28.0%Edge Hitter

Jake Burger RHB vs Peter Lambert RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 586+1324+2089, pitcher rows 478+0+1044

Verdict

Lambert's slurve is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake Burger should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.370 xwOBA (+55 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +26 pts (raw 0.327 -> adj 0.353); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.353 -> final 0.370); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jake Burger is on a 14-day heater (+78 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

18.Lenyn SosaRHBTOR vs Jack Flaherty (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.322 / xBA 0.232 / xSLG 0.409 (+7 pts)Park +4 pts14d —vs RHP —K 28.8%BB 3.3%HR 6.0%Hit 23.8%Even

Lenyn Sosa RHB vs Jack Flaherty RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 326+2056+1384, pitcher rows 786+3028+3034

Verdict

Flaherty's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Lenyn Sosa should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.322 xwOBA (+7 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.307 -> adj 0.301); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.301 -> final 0.322); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 19.Brice MatthewsRHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.324 / xBA 0.215 / xSLG 0.342 (+9 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.259vs RHP 0.264K 29.0%BB 4.0%HR 6.0%Hit 23.7%Even

Brice Matthews RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 479+179+0, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.324 xwOBA (+9 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.291 -> adj 0.274); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +57 pts (bb_type-adj 0.274 -> final 0.324); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

21.Luke RaleyLHBSEA vs Lucas Giolito (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.361 / xBA 0.248 / xSLG 0.490 (+46 pts)Park -11 pts14d 0.517vs RHP 0.531K 26.5%BB 8.2%HR 5.9%Hit 24.8%Slight Hitter

Luke Raley LHB vs Lucas Giolito RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 487+850+1714, pitcher rows 0+2372+0

Verdict

Giolito's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Luke Raley should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.361 xwOBA (+46 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +25 pts (bb_type-adj 0.347 -> final 0.361); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Luke Raley is on a 14-day heater (+114 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

22.Willy AdamesRHBSF vs Jeffrey Springs (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.369 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.441 (+54 pts)Park +22 pts14d 0.292vs LHP —K 23.5%BB 9.0%HR 5.9%Hit 24.7%Edge Hitter

Willy Adames RHB vs Jeffrey Springs LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 690+2807+2804, pitcher rows 785+2849+565

Verdict

Springs' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Willy Adames should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.369 xwOBA (+54 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.332); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.332 -> final 0.369); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jeffrey Springs is in a 14-day rough patch (+56 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

23.Justin FoscueRHBTEX vs Peter Lambert (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.348 / xBA 0.276 / xSLG 0.462 (+33 pts)Park -1 pts14d —vs RHP —K 17.0%BB 6.6%HR 5.7%Hit 20.5%Slight Hitter

Justin Foscue RHB vs Peter Lambert RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 53+42+187, pitcher rows 478+0+1044

Verdict

Lambert's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Justin Foscue should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.348 xwOBA (+33 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +28 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.349); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Trent Grisham LHB vs Freddy Peralta RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 663+2529+857, pitcher rows 762+3365+3151

Verdict

Peralta's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Trent Grisham should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.350 xwOBA (+35 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.336 -> adj 0.351); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Changeup25.30.3120.284GB 55% / LD 21% / FB 19% / PU 5%0.314+20.2830.2370.32331.9

Sweeper0.20.3150.316GB 37% / LD 26% / FB 27% / PU 10%0.319+40.3200.2520.40344.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 68.5 mph
swing length 7.5 ft
attack angle 15.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.9+14.2186+10.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-6.8+20.470+13.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-8.9+15.725+6.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-7.6+13.445+5.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slow Curve-10.0+15.1*0+5.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-8.2+15.810+7.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

25.Christian YelichLHBMIL vs Bailey Ober (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.362 / xBA 0.227 / xSLG 0.507 (+47 pts)Park +6 pts14d —vs RHP —K 19.7%BB 5.8%HR 5.7%Hit 27.1%Slight Hitter

Christian Yelich LHB vs Bailey Ober RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 248+2775+1275, pitcher rows 776+2356+2697

Verdict

Ober's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Yelich should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.362 xwOBA (+47 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.336); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.336 -> final 0.362); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bailey Ober is in a 14-day hot stretch (-77 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

26.Alex BregmanRHBCHC vs Erick Fedde (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.346 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.413 (+31 pts)Park +30 pts14d 0.318vs RHP 0.311K 12.6%BB 6.8%HR 5.7%Hit 23.6%Slight Hitter

Alex Bregman RHB vs Erick Fedde RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 753+2018+2437, pitcher rows 675+2374+2895

Verdict

Fedde's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Alex Bregman should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.346 xwOBA (+31 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.313); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Erick Fedde is in a 14-day rough patch (+103 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

27.Tyler O'NeillRHBBAL vs Richard Lovelady (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.337 / xBA 0.264 / xSLG 0.358 (+22 pts)Park -2 pts14d 0.240vs LHP —K 24.5%BB 13.3%HR 5.6%Hit 18.8%Even

Tyler O'Neill RHB vs Richard Lovelady LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 284+827+2003, pitcher rows 323+222+532

Verdict

Lovelady's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler O'Neill should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.337 xwOBA (+22 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.325 -> adj 0.309); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +30 pts (bb_type-adj 0.309 -> final 0.337); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Tyler O'Neill is on a 14-day slump (-45 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Kolek's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Nathan Church should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.359 xwOBA (+44 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +42 pts (bb_type-adj 0.318 -> final 0.359); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

29.Mickey MoniakLHBCOL vs Michael Soroka (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.359 / xBA 0.250 / xSLG 0.394 (+44 pts)Park +26 pts14d 0.348vs RHP 0.387K 23.7%BB 6.4%HR 5.5%Hit 24.5%Slight Hitter

Mickey Moniak LHB vs Michael Soroka RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 509+1602+1534, pitcher rows 729+1572+1447

Verdict

Soroka's slurve is the projected matchup advantage, while Mickey Moniak should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.359 xwOBA (+44 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.324 -> adj 0.309); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.309 -> final 0.359); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

30.Michael Harris IILHBATL vs Brayan Bello (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.390 / xBA 0.294 / xSLG 0.520 (+75 pts)Park -16 pts14d 0.277vs RHP —K 14.7%BB 5.0%HR 5.5%Hit 31.2%Edge Hitter

Michael Harris II LHB vs Brayan Bello RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 534+2181+1718, pitcher rows 686+2726+2713

Verdict

Bello's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Harris II should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.390 xwOBA (+75 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +33 pts (raw 0.345 -> adj 0.377); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.377 -> final 0.390); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Harris II is on a 14-day slump (-122 pts vs season). Brayan Bello is in a 14-day hot stretch (-101 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

31.Pete Crow-ArmstrongLHBCHC vs Erick Fedde (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.399 / xBA 0.283 / xSLG 0.450 (+84 pts)Park +28 pts14d 0.326vs RHP 0.370K 14.5%BB 7.8%HR 5.5%Hit 27.8%Edge Hitter

Pete Crow-Armstrong LHB vs Erick Fedde RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 641+2403+1424, pitcher rows 675+2374+2895

Verdict

Fedde's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Pete Crow-Armstrong should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.399 xwOBA (+84 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.348 -> final 0.399); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Erick Fedde is in a 14-day rough patch (+103 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

32.Zach ColeLHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.280 / xBA 0.246 / xSLG 0.415 (-35 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.339vs RHP 0.390K 26.6%BB 3.5%HR 5.5%Hit 19.0%Slight Pitcher

Zach Cole LHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 111+224+0, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Cole should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.280 xwOBA (-35 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.280); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

33.Nick KurtzLHBATH vs Adrian Houser (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.420 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.484 (+105 pts)Park +29 pts14d 0.404vs RHP 0.498K 18.0%BB 18.4%HR 5.5%Hit 23.0%Strong Hitter

Nick Kurtz LHB vs Adrian Houser RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 860+1965+0, pitcher rows 661+1892+1131

Verdict

Projection: 0.420 xwOBA (+105 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.376 -> adj 0.363); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.363 -> final 0.420); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adrian Houser is in a 14-day hot stretch (-40 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

34.Oneil CruzLHBPIT vs Zack Wheeler (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.373 / xBA 0.221 / xSLG 0.452 (+58 pts)14d 0.355vs RHP 0.381K 33.4%BB 8.7%HR 5.5%Hit 24.7%Edge Hitter

Oneil Cruz LHB vs Zack Wheeler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 764+2250+2404, pitcher rows 363+2394+3247

Verdict

Wheeler's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Oneil Cruz should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.373 xwOBA (+58 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +47 pts (bb_type-adj 0.327 -> final 0.373); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

35.Jackson ChourioRHBMIL vs Bailey Ober (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.325 / xBA 0.225 / xSLG 0.416 (+10 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.305vs RHP 0.350K 22.7%BB 3.9%HR 5.4%Hit 23.4%Even

Jackson Chourio RHB vs Bailey Ober RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 176+2228+2163, pitcher rows 776+2356+2697

Verdict

Ober's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Chourio should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.325 xwOBA (+10 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.308 -> adj 0.301); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.301 -> final 0.325); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bailey Ober is in a 14-day hot stretch (-77 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 71.0 mph
swing length 7.3 ft
attack angle 6.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.8+3.6203-1.2on plane
Changeup-7.9+13.124+5.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-7.3+9.9111+2.6on plane
Sweeper-8.7+9.687+0.8on plane
Sinker-6.1+3.0131-3.0swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Curveball-10.2+13.126+2.8on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

36.Dalton RushingLHBLAD vs Grayson Rodriguez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.314 / xBA 0.226 / xSLG 0.389 (-1 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.183vs RHP —K 32.5%BB 7.4%HR 5.4%Hit 21.2%Even

Dalton Rushing LHB vs Grayson Rodriguez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 270+604+0, pitcher rows 0+0+1879

Verdict

Rodriguez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Dalton Rushing should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.314 xwOBA (-1 pts vs league, Even). Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.302 -> final 0.314); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Dalton Rushing is on a 14-day slump (-165 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

37.Braden ShewmakeLHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.345 / xBA 0.270 / xSLG 0.430 (+30 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.270vs RHP —K 21.0%BB 3.2%HR 5.3%Hit 28.0%Slight Hitter

Braden Shewmake LHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 119+0+214, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Braden Shewmake should look to drive the split-finger. Projection: 0.345 xwOBA (+30 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.312 -> adj 0.327); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.327 -> final 0.345); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 67.3 mph
swing length 6.9 ft
attack angle 8.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Split-Finger-7.5+17.85+10.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+5.130+0.3on plane
Cutter-5.4+2.36-3.0swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Curveball-9.6+8.4*4-1.2on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 38.Yordan AlvarezLHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.351 / xBA 0.262 / xSLG 0.461 (+36 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.446vs RHP 0.381K 18.5%BB 5.9%HR 5.3%Hit 25.4%Slight Hitter

Yordan Alvarez LHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 768+824+2359, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Yordan Alvarez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.351 xwOBA (+36 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.337 -> final 0.351); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

39.Fernando Tatis Jr.RHBSD vs George Kirby (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.367 / xBA 0.287 / xSLG 0.510 (+52 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.294vs RHP 0.308K 21.4%BB 3.6%HR 5.3%Hit 28.9%Edge Hitter

Fernando Tatis Jr. RHB vs George Kirby RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 674+2699+1628, pitcher rows 830+2386+2969

Verdict

Kirby's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Fernando Tatis Jr. should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.367 xwOBA (+52 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +25 pts (raw 0.341 -> adj 0.366); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.366 -> final 0.367); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Fernando Tatis Jr. is on a 14-day slump (-46 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

40.Jo AdellRHBLAA vs Roki Sasaki (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.342 / xBA 0.248 / xSLG 0.410 (+27 pts)Park -11 pts14d 0.386vs RHP —K 21.6%BB 5.2%HR 5.3%Hit 19.2%Slight Hitter

Jo Adell RHB vs Roki Sasaki RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 701+2221+1845, pitcher rows 634+733+0

Verdict

Sasaki's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Jo Adell should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.342 xwOBA (+27 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.342 -> adj 0.316); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +37 pts (bb_type-adj 0.316 -> final 0.342); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jo Adell is on a 14-day heater (+65 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

41.Corbin CarrollLHBAZ vs Michael Lorenzen (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.409 / xBA 0.281 / xSLG 0.479 (+94 pts)Park +30 pts14d 0.352vs RHP 0.364K 19.5%BB 11.5%HR 5.3%Hit 24.7%Edge Hitter

Corbin Carroll LHB vs Michael Lorenzen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 660+2514+2649, pitcher rows 787+2318+2134

Verdict

Lorenzen's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Corbin Carroll should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.409 xwOBA (+94 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +22 pts (raw 0.343 -> adj 0.366); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.366 -> final 0.409); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Lovelady's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Coby Mayo should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.347 xwOBA (+32 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.333); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.333 -> final 0.347); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 467+1766+1981, pitcher rows 174+0+0

Verdict

Projection: 0.369 xwOBA (+54 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.341 -> final 0.369); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mark Vientos is on a 14-day heater (+193 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker55.00.3350.331GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.348+130.3650.2880.4755.3

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

44.Ben RiceLHBNYY vs Freddy Peralta (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.358 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.453 (+43 pts)Park +8 pts14d 0.325vs RHP —K 22.4%BB 12.0%HR 5.2%Hit 21.2%Slight Hitter

Ben Rice LHB vs Freddy Peralta RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 652+2129+725, pitcher rows 762+3365+3151

Verdict

Peralta's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Ben Rice should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.358 xwOBA (+43 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.354 -> adj 0.336); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.336 -> final 0.358); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ben Rice is on a 14-day slump (-97 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

45.Brandon LoweLHBPIT vs Zack Wheeler (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.333 / xBA 0.215 / xSLG 0.412 (+18 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.341vs RHP 0.361K 29.1%BB 8.3%HR 5.2%Hit 22.1%Even

Brandon Lowe LHB vs Zack Wheeler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 710+2148+1632, pitcher rows 363+2394+3247

Verdict

Wheeler's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Brandon Lowe should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.333 xwOBA (+18 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.327 -> adj 0.306); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.306 -> final 0.333); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball46.50.3630.304GB 27% / LD 24% / FB 38% / PU 11%0.343-210.3310.2160.46829.6
Cutter16.20.3330.308GB 46% / LD 17% / FB 28% / PU 9%0.287-460.2800.2050.36017.0
Curveball14.90.3200.278GB 51% / LD 27% / FB 20% / PU 1%0.300-200.2630.2080.30054.5
Split-Finger11.90.2940.301GB 51% / LD 24% / FB 20% / PU 6%0.305+110.2910.2310.37251.4
Sweeper5.70.3190.314GB 26% / LD 22% / FB 45% / PU 7%0.344+250.3430.2310.49249.0
Sinker4.90.3580.305GB 47% / LD 14% / FB 33% / PU 6%0.291-670.2800.1990.4005.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 71.6 mph
swing length 7.1 ft
attack angle 13.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.0+6.1215+2.2on plane
Cutter-4.9+12.963+8.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-9.4+18.758+9.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Split-Finger-7.1+19.157+12.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-7.2+20.736+13.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.3+7.365+2.0on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

46.Julio RodríguezRHBSEA vs Lucas Giolito (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.357 / xBA 0.272 / xSLG 0.456 (+42 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.315vs RHP 0.284K 17.6%BB 6.4%HR 5.1%Hit 26.3%Slight Hitter

Julio Rodríguez RHB vs Lucas Giolito RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 752+2727+2389, pitcher rows 0+2372+0

Verdict

Giolito's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Julio Rodríguez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.357 xwOBA (+42 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.349 -> adj 0.343); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.343 -> final 0.357); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

47.Rafael DeversLHBSF vs Jeffrey Springs (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.358 / xBA 0.235 / xSLG 0.404 (+43 pts)Park +18 pts14d 0.268vs LHP —K 23.9%BB 5.7%HR 5.1%Hit 26.7%Slight Hitter

Rafael Devers LHB vs Jeffrey Springs LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 729+3102+2433, pitcher rows 785+2849+565

Verdict

Springs' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Rafael Devers should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.358 xwOBA (+43 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +30 pts (bb_type-adj 0.310 -> final 0.358); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jeffrey Springs is in a 14-day rough patch (+56 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

49.Ian HappRHBCHC vs Erick Fedde (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.368 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.427 (+53 pts)Park +26 pts14d 0.427vs RHP 0.455K 19.1%BB 11.4%HR 5.1%Hit 23.5%Edge Hitter

Ian Happ RHB vs Erick Fedde RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 786+2839+2728, pitcher rows 675+2374+2895

Verdict

Fedde's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Ian Happ should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.368 xwOBA (+53 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.344 -> adj 0.322); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.322 -> final 0.368); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ian Happ is on a 14-day heater (+64 pts vs season). Erick Fedde is in a 14-day rough patch (+103 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker39.10.3730.341GB 46% / LD 30% / FB 21% / PU 2%0.365-80.3900.2960.4985.5
Sweeper35.10.3120.284GB 36% / LD 14% / FB 34% / PU 16%0.260-520.2290.1610.30031.5

4-Seam Fastball0.40.3530.315GB 40% / LD 21% / FB 30% / PU 9%0.329-240.3290.2340.42314.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

50.Max MuncyLHBLAD vs Grayson Rodriguez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.325 / xBA 0.235 / xSLG 0.407 (+10 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.377vs RHP 0.426K 26.1%BB 14.8%HR 5.0%Hit 17.4%Even

Max Muncy LHB vs Grayson Rodriguez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 641+1879+1447, pitcher rows 0+0+1879

Verdict

Rodriguez's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Max Muncy should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.325 xwOBA (+10 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.346 -> adj 0.322); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.322 -> final 0.325); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

51.Carter JensenLHBKC vs Andre Pallante (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.323 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.326 (+8 pts)Park -3 pts14d 0.252vs RHP 0.256K 20.5%BB 12.6%HR 5.0%Hit 18.2%Even

Carter Jensen LHB vs Andre Pallante RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 608+293+0, pitcher rows 728+2722+1981

Verdict

Pallante's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Carter Jensen should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.323 xwOBA (+8 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -41 pts (raw 0.329 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +38 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.323); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Carter Jensen is on a 14-day slump (-50 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball63.20.3240.341GB 71% / LD 16% / FB 12% / PU 1%0.277-470.3030.2550.33123.6
Knuckle Curve17.60.3140.271GB 53% / LD 17% / FB 27% / PU 3%0.282-320.2380.1960.29239.9

Split-Finger1.30.3170.312GB 65% / LD 18% / FB 15% / PU 2%0.275-420.2720.2320.30639.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

52.Kyle StowersLHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.318 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.385 (+3 pts)Park -11 pts14d 0.344vs RHP 0.377K 28.4%BB 7.3%HR 5.0%Hit 21.5%Even

Kyle Stowers LHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 344+1724+821, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Stowers should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.318 xwOBA (+3 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.323 -> adj 0.306); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.306 -> final 0.318); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

53.Bryce HarperLHBPHI vs Paul Skenes (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.304 / xBA 0.220 / xSLG 0.379 (-11 pts)Park -2 pts14d 0.463vs RHP 0.499K 26.8%BB 8.6%HR 4.9%Hit 18.6%Even

Bryce Harper LHB vs Paul Skenes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 673+2264+2593, pitcher rows 706+2997+2127

Verdict

Skenes' split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Harper should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.304 xwOBA (-11 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.315 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.304); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bryce Harper is on a 14-day heater (+58 pts vs season). Paul Skenes is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Peralta's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jazz Chisholm Jr. should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.349 xwOBA (+34 pts vs league, Hitter). Jazz Chisholm Jr. is on a 14-day slump (-42 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

55.Andrew BenintendiLHBCWS vs Colin Rea (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.394 / xBA 0.278 / xSLG 0.453 (+79 pts)Park +33 pts14d 0.367vs RHP —K 16.1%BB 8.5%HR 4.9%Hit 28.8%Edge Hitter

Andrew Benintendi LHB vs Colin Rea RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 512+1729+2001, pitcher rows 729+2657+2656

Verdict

Rea's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Andrew Benintendi should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.394 xwOBA (+79 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.343 -> adj 0.349); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.349 -> final 0.394); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Andrew Benintendi is on a 14-day heater (+64 pts vs season). Colin Rea is in a 14-day rough patch (+96 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

56.Konnor GriffinRHBPIT vs Zack Wheeler (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.294 / xBA 0.215 / xSLG 0.377 (-21 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.356vs RHP 0.308K 31.7%BB 3.4%HR 4.8%Hit 23.7%Even

Konnor Griffin RHB vs Zack Wheeler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 579+0+0, pitcher rows 363+2394+3247

Verdict

Wheeler's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Konnor Griffin should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.294 xwOBA (-21 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.284 -> final 0.294); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Konnor Griffin is on a 14-day heater (+41 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 73.3 mph
swing length 7.6 ft
attack angle 8.0°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-5.4+2.536-2.8on plane
4-Seam Fastball-3.9+2.625-1.3on plane
Sweeper-7.0+12.313+5.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Split-Finger-7.2+8.0*1+0.7on plane
Curveball-9.6+8.0*5-1.6on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

57.Gavin SheetsLHBSD vs George Kirby (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.305 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.381 (-10 pts)Park -9 pts14d 0.332vs RHP 0.320K 22.5%BB 6.2%HR 4.8%Hit 20.9%Even

Gavin Sheets LHB vs George Kirby RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 524+2301+2094, pitcher rows 830+2386+2969

Verdict

Kirby's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Gavin Sheets should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.305 xwOBA (-10 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.305); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

58.Logan O'HoppeRHBLAA vs Roki Sasaki (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.341 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.390 (+26 pts)Park -7 pts14d —vs RHP —K 23.6%BB 5.5%HR 4.8%Hit 22.7%Slight Hitter

Logan O'Hoppe RHB vs Roki Sasaki RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 384+1758+1985, pitcher rows 634+733+0

Verdict

Sasaki's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Logan O'Hoppe should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.341 xwOBA (+26 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -20 pts (raw 0.326 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +42 pts (bb_type-adj 0.305 -> final 0.341); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.
