Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

59.Jonathan ArandaLHBTB vs Eury Pérez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.347 / xBA 0.232 / xSLG 0.443 (+32 pts)Park -14 pts14d 0.424vs RHP 0.474K 27.0%BB 10.5%HR 4.7%Hit 22.6%Slight Hitter

Jonathan Aranda LHB vs Eury Pérez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 728+1732+574, pitcher rows 803+1675+0

Verdict

Pérez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jonathan Aranda should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.347 xwOBA (+32 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.342 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +31 pts (bb_type-adj 0.330 -> final 0.347); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jonathan Aranda is on a 14-day heater (+68 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

60.Mike TroutRHBLAA vs Roki Sasaki (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.354 / xBA 0.253 / xSLG 0.419 (+39 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.282vs RHP 0.331K 23.0%BB 12.3%HR 4.7%Hit 18.3%Slight Hitter

Mike Trout RHB vs Roki Sasaki RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 814+2511+578, pitcher rows 634+733+0

Verdict

Sasaki's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Mike Trout should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.354 xwOBA (+39 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.350 -> adj 0.339); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.339 -> final 0.354); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mike Trout is on a 14-day slump (-120 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball47.50.3480.360GB 49% / LD 22% / FB 26% / PU 3%0.337-110.3820.2730.47910.5
Forkball22.00.3150.305GB 58% / LD 22% / FB 16% / PU 4%0.315+00.3060.2300.369100.0

Sinker1.10.3610.315GB 53% / LD 21% / FB 22% / PU 5%0.342-180.3420.2490.45326.5

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Verdict

Lambert's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Higashioka should look to drive the slurve. Projection: 0.336 xwOBA (+21 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +26 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.345); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

62.Daniel SchneemannLHBCLE vs Brady Singer (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.366 / xBA 0.257 / xSLG 0.432 (+51 pts)Park +13 pts14d 0.310vs RHP 0.305K 25.3%BB 9.4%HR 4.7%Hit 25.4%Edge Hitter

Daniel Schneemann LHB vs Brady Singer RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 522+1678+934, pitcher rows 734+2850+2975

Verdict

Singer's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Daniel Schneemann should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.366 xwOBA (+51 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.335 -> final 0.366); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Verdict

Pérez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Cedric Mullins should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.298 xwOBA (-17 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.295 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 64.Zach NetoRHBLAA vs Roki Sasaki (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.344 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.416 (+29 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.338vs RHP 0.289K 21.5%BB 6.1%HR 4.6%Hit 18.1%Slight Hitter

Zach Neto RHB vs Roki Sasaki RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 815+2197+2295, pitcher rows 634+733+0

Verdict

Sasaki's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach Neto should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.344 xwOBA (+29 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.338 -> adj 0.329); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.329 -> final 0.344); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

65.Will BensonLHBCIN vs Gavin Williams (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.339 / xBA 0.227 / xSLG 0.403 (+24 pts)Park +11 pts14d 0.375vs RHP 0.375K 32.0%BB 11.5%HR 4.6%Hit 21.0%Even

Will Benson LHB vs Gavin Williams RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 366+938+1658, pitcher rows 858+3013+1408

Verdict

Williams' curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Will Benson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.339 xwOBA (+24 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.323 -> adj 0.310); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.310 -> final 0.339); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Will Benson is on a 14-day heater (+52 pts vs season). Gavin Williams is in a 14-day rough patch (+63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

66.Heliot RamosRHBSF vs Jeffrey Springs (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.362 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.433 (+47 pts)Park +16 pts14d 0.298vs LHP —K 21.7%BB 7.0%HR 4.5%Hit 25.1%Slight Hitter

Heliot Ramos RHB vs Jeffrey Springs LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 681+2665+2019, pitcher rows 785+2849+565

Verdict

Springs' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Heliot Ramos should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.362 xwOBA (+47 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.333 -> adj 0.317); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.317 -> final 0.362); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jeffrey Springs is in a 14-day rough patch (+56 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

67.CJ AbramsLHBWSH vs Brandon Young (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.348 / xBA 0.236 / xSLG 0.425 (+33 pts)Park +12 pts14d 0.295vs RHP 0.333K 11.9%BB 9.6%HR 4.5%Hit 23.0%Slight Hitter

CJ Abrams LHB vs Brandon Young RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 692+2324+2273, pitcher rows 431+941+0

Verdict

Young's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while CJ Abrams should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.348 xwOBA (+33 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.319 -> final 0.348); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. CJ Abrams is on a 14-day slump (-66 pts vs season). Brandon Young is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch Out0.10.3330.321GB 39% / LD 21% / FB 30% / PU 10%0.326-60.3330.2420.45316.4

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 70.8 mph
swing length 7.5 ft
attack angle 10.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.5+6.3232+1.7on plane
Split-Finger-7.5+14.236+6.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-10.8+14.464+3.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-6.7+13.898+7.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-7.0+10.060+3.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-4.9+5.965+1.0on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

68.Nolan GormanLHBSTL vs Stephen Kolek (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.333 / xBA 0.237 / xSLG 0.345 (+18 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.383vs RHP 0.400K 26.3%BB 9.4%HR 4.5%Hit 22.4%Even

Nolan Gorman LHB vs Stephen Kolek RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 657+1663+1601, pitcher rows 177+1693+786

Verdict

Kolek's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Nolan Gorman should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.333 xwOBA (+18 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -35 pts (raw 0.323 -> adj 0.288); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +41 pts (bb_type-adj 0.288 -> final 0.333); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nolan Gorman is on a 14-day heater (+68 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

69.Salvador PerezRHBKC vs Andre Pallante (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.327 / xBA 0.249 / xSLG 0.392 (+12 pts)14d 0.248vs RHP 0.193K 15.1%BB 4.9%HR 4.4%Hit 23.1%Even

Salvador Perez RHB vs Andre Pallante RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 669+2287+2405, pitcher rows 728+2722+1981

Verdict

Pallante's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Salvador Perez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.327 xwOBA (+12 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -33 pts (raw 0.337 -> adj 0.304); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.304 -> final 0.327); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Slider34.80.3260.295GB 53% / LD 21% / FB 22% / PU 4%0.286-400.2650.2180.34342.0

Pitch Out0.10.3370.308GB 55% / LD 19% / FB 22% / PU 4%0.302-350.2950.2420.39320.3

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Verdict

Kolek's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while JJ Wetherholt should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.323 xwOBA (+8 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.329 -> adj 0.306); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.306 -> final 0.323); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. 71.Riley GreeneLHBDET vs Kevin Gausman (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.350 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.453 (+35 pts)Park -1 pts14d 0.369vs RHP 0.386K 25.1%BB 6.8%HR 4.4%Hit 25.8%Slight Hitter

Riley Greene LHB vs Kevin Gausman RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 739+2826+2545, pitcher rows 820+3482+2959

Verdict

Gausman's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Riley Greene should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.350 xwOBA (+35 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.336 -> adj 0.321); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +30 pts (bb_type-adj 0.321 -> final 0.350); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball57.00.3580.344GB 26% / LD 26% / FB 39% / PU 9%0.356-10.3850.2670.57911.3
Split-Finger39.90.3150.261GB 52% / LD 25% / FB 18% / PU 5%0.280-350.2270.2000.27258.5
Slider2.70.3300.343GB 37% / LD 26% / FB 29% / PU 8%0.312-180.3400.2680.45422.6
Sinker0.40.3360.316GB 37% / LD 26% / FB 29% / PU 8%0.368+320.3690.2580.5273.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 643+2004+1217, pitcher rows 830+2386+2969

Verdict

Kirby's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Ramón Laureano should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.325 xwOBA (+10 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.322 -> adj 0.312); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.312 -> final 0.325); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

73.Andy PagesRHBLAD vs Grayson Rodriguez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.329 / xBA 0.257 / xSLG 0.408 (+14 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.386vs RHP 0.436K 23.6%BB 4.1%HR 4.4%Hit 24.0%Even

Andy Pages RHB vs Grayson Rodriguez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 718+2543+1810, pitcher rows 0+0+1879

Verdict

Rodriguez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Andy Pages should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.329 xwOBA (+14 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.318 -> final 0.329); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

74.Matt OlsonLHBATL vs Brayan Bello (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.381 / xBA 0.274 / xSLG 0.477 (+66 pts)Park -10 pts14d 0.418vs RHP —K 17.3%BB 15.3%HR 4.3%Hit 22.5%Edge Hitter

Matt Olson LHB vs Brayan Bello RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 861+3172+3000, pitcher rows 686+2726+2713

Verdict

Bello's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Olson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.381 xwOBA (+66 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.365 -> final 0.381); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brayan Bello is in a 14-day hot stretch (-101 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 75.Cody BellingerLHBNYY vs Freddy Peralta (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.331 / xBA 0.248 / xSLG 0.402 (+16 pts)14d 0.427vs RHP 0.406K 18.4%BB 10.8%HR 4.3%Hit 21.3%Even

Cody Bellinger LHB vs Freddy Peralta RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 677+2697+2109, pitcher rows 762+3365+3151

Verdict

Peralta's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Cody Bellinger should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.331 xwOBA (+16 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.321 -> final 0.331); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cody Bellinger is on a 14-day heater (+47 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

76.Ryan KreidlerRHBMIN vs Robert Gasser (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.314 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.390 (-1 pts)Park +5 pts14d —vs LHP —K 12.2%BB 4.2%HR 4.2%Hit 24.8%Even

Ryan Kreidler RHB vs Robert Gasser LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 105+179+263, pitcher rows 0+166+399

Verdict

Gasser's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Ryan Kreidler should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.314 xwOBA (-1 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.318 -> adj 0.308); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sweeper-6.8+14.7*1+7.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

77.Miguel VargasRHBCWS vs Colin Rea (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.339 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.398 (+24 pts)Park +30 pts14d 0.517vs RHP 0.502K 15.9%BB 5.9%HR 4.2%Hit 24.3%Even

Miguel Vargas RHB vs Colin Rea RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 730+2326+1009, pitcher rows 729+2657+2656

Verdict

Rea's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Miguel Vargas should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.339 xwOBA (+24 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.325 -> adj 0.313); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Miguel Vargas is on a 14-day heater (+114 pts vs season). Colin Rea is in a 14-day rough patch (+96 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

78.Christian WalkerRHBHOU vs Nathan Eovaldi (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.271 / xBA 0.211 / xSLG 0.308 (-44 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.212vs RHP 0.217K 27.6%BB 4.1%HR 4.2%Hit 18.3%Slight Pitcher

Christian Walker RHB vs Nathan Eovaldi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 678+2519+2326, pitcher rows 649+1924+2623

Verdict

Eovaldi's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Walker should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.271 xwOBA (-44 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -35 pts (raw 0.296 -> adj 0.261); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.261 -> final 0.271); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Walker is on a 14-day slump (-121 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

4-Seam Fastball27.20.3300.296GB 58% / LD 21% / FB 17% / PU 4%0.304-260.2850.2340.33412.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 71.8 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 8.2°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Split-Finger-7.6+16.520+8.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+3.4287-1.3on plane
Curveball-9.5+16.244+6.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.7+8.159+2.4on plane
Sinker-5.2+4.1156-1.1on plane
Slider-7.4+12.3141+4.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

79.Jackson MerrillLHBSD vs George Kirby (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.315 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.387 (-0 pts)Park -15 pts14d 0.349vs RHP 0.408K 23.6%BB 5.0%HR 4.2%Hit 23.9%Even

Jackson Merrill LHB vs George Kirby RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 692+1748+2291, pitcher rows 830+2386+2969

Verdict

Kirby's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Merrill should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (-0 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.321 -> adj 0.300); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +30 pts (bb_type-adj 0.300 -> final 0.315); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

bat speed 70.9 mph

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.3+4.0262-0.3on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

80.Christopher MorelRHBMIA vs Drew Rasmussen (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.308 / xBA 0.246 / xSLG 0.379 (-7 pts)Park -10 pts14d 0.196vs RHP —K 25.1%BB 4.7%HR 4.2%Hit 23.4%Even

Christopher Morel RHB vs Drew Rasmussen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 135+1178+2364, pitcher rows 603+2317+460

Verdict

Rasmussen's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Christopher Morel should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.308 xwOBA (-7 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.302); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.302 -> final 0.308); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Rasmussen is in a 14-day rough patch (+44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

82.Dansby SwansonRHBCHC vs Erick Fedde (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.379 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.455 (+64 pts)Park +26 pts14d 0.235vs RHP 0.221K 18.7%BB 7.2%HR 4.1%Hit 28.6%Edge Hitter

Dansby Swanson RHB vs Erick Fedde RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 677+2625+2365, pitcher rows 675+2374+2895

Verdict

Fedde's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Dansby Swanson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.379 xwOBA (+64 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.329 -> adj 0.337); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.337 -> final 0.379); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Dansby Swanson is on a 14-day slump (-80 pts vs season). Erick Fedde is in a 14-day rough patch (+103 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

84.Jonah HeimRHBATH vs Adrian Houser (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.315 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.354 (-0 pts)Park +15 pts14d —vs RHP —K 14.1%BB 3.7%HR 4.1%Hit 25.4%Even

Jonah Heim RHB vs Adrian Houser RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 201+1704+1783, pitcher rows 661+1892+1131

Verdict

Houser's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jonah Heim should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (-0 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.291); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.291 -> final 0.315); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adrian Houser is in a 14-day hot stretch (-40 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker57.10.3240.306GB 62% / LD 21% / FB 13% / PU 4%0.315-90.3060.2620.3565.4
Slider22.20.3020.300GB 51% / LD 17% / FB 23% / PU 9%0.293-80.2790.2280.37516.6
4-Seam Fastball7.90.2890.295GB 38% / LD 10% / FB 43% / PU 9%0.244-460.2230.1620.27027.7
Changeup6.40.2860.303GB 56% / LD 20% / FB 18% / PU 6%0.296+100.2840.2220.36836.6
Curveball6.40.3060.308GB 56% / LD 20% / FB 18% / PU 6%0.300-60.2930.2410.35521.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

85.Spencer SteerRHBCIN vs Gavin Williams (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.356 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.408 (+41 pts)Park +7 pts14d 0.414vs RHP 0.367K 22.8%BB 10.6%HR 4.1%Hit 23.6%Slight Hitter

Spencer Steer RHB vs Gavin Williams RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 629+2347+2630, pitcher rows 858+3013+1408

Verdict

Williams' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Spencer Steer should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.356 xwOBA (+41 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.322 -> adj 0.331); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.331 -> final 0.356); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Gavin Williams is in a 14-day rough patch (+63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

86.Tyler SoderstromLHBATH vs Adrian Houser (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.423 / xBA 0.286 / xSLG 0.520 (+108 pts)Park +24 pts14d 0.327vs RHP 0.345K 14.6%BB 12.3%HR 4.1%Hit 28.1%Strong Hitter

Tyler Soderstrom LHB vs Adrian Houser RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 641+2238+773, pitcher rows 661+1892+1131

Verdict

Projection: 0.423 xwOBA (+108 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +31 pts (raw 0.351 -> adj 0.382); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.382 -> final 0.423); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adrian Houser is in a 14-day hot stretch (-40 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

87.Matt ChapmanRHBSF vs Jeffrey Springs (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.379 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.464 (+64 pts)Park +17 pts14d 0.204vs LHP —K 19.6%BB 10.2%HR 4.1%Hit 27.1%Edge Hitter

Matt Chapman RHB vs Jeffrey Springs LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 701+2213+2722, pitcher rows 785+2849+565

Verdict

Springs' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Chapman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.379 xwOBA (+64 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.329 -> adj 0.344); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.344 -> final 0.379); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Matt Chapman is on a 14-day slump (-46 pts vs season). Jeffrey Springs is in a 14-day rough patch (+56 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

88.Manny MachadoRHBSD vs George Kirby (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.322 / xBA 0.261 / xSLG 0.439 (+7 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.253vs RHP 0.224K 22.0%BB 2.6%HR 4.1%Hit 26.3%Even

Manny Machado RHB vs George Kirby RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 703+2714+2490, pitcher rows 830+2386+2969

Verdict

Kirby's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Manny Machado should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.322 xwOBA (+7 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.320 -> final 0.322); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Manny Machado is on a 14-day slump (-45 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

89.Michael BuschLHBCHC vs Erick Fedde (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.382 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.436 (+67 pts)Park +23 pts14d 0.380vs RHP 0.405K 15.3%BB 13.5%HR 4.1%Hit 24.3%Edge Hitter

Michael Busch LHB vs Erick Fedde RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 723+2419+2369, pitcher rows 675+2374+2895

Verdict

Fedde's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Busch should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.382 xwOBA (+67 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.358 -> adj 0.340); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.340 -> final 0.382); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Busch is on a 14-day heater (+60 pts vs season). Erick Fedde is in a 14-day rough patch (+103 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 90.Lawrence ButlerLHBATH vs Adrian Houser (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.390 / xBA 0.262 / xSLG 0.474 (+75 pts)Park +24 pts14d 0.270vs RHP 0.266K 17.2%BB 13.2%HR 4.0%Hit 25.9%Edge Hitter

Lawrence Butler LHB vs Adrian Houser RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 597+2521+1816, pitcher rows 661+1892+1131

Verdict

Houser's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Lawrence Butler should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.390 xwOBA (+75 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.345 -> adj 0.354); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.354 -> final 0.390); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adrian Houser is in a 14-day hot stretch (-40 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

91.Ryan McMahonLHBNYY vs Freddy Peralta (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.341 / xBA 0.231 / xSLG 0.405 (+26 pts)14d 0.256vs RHP 0.252K 29.6%BB 12.6%HR 4.0%Hit 21.3%Slight Hitter

Ryan McMahon LHB vs Freddy Peralta RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 488+2572+2741, pitcher rows 762+3365+3151

Verdict

Peralta's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Ryan McMahon should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.341 xwOBA (+26 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.334 -> adj 0.313); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.313 -> final 0.341); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball55.40.3490.340GB 30% / LD 26% / FB 31% / PU 12%0.330-190.3550.2490.48118.9

Sweeper0.20.3250.316GB 37% / LD 26% / FB 27% / PU 10%0.315-100.3160.2400.40774.7

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 71.9 mph
swing length 7.3 ft
attack angle 10.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.9+4.3294+0.3on plane
Changeup-6.8+17.894+11.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

92.Dillon DinglerRHBDET vs Kevin Gausman (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.331 / xBA 0.246 / xSLG 0.422 (+16 pts)Park +2 pts14d 0.304vs RHP 0.321K 24.7%BB 3.7%HR 4.0%Hit 26.6%Even

Dillon Dingler RHB vs Kevin Gausman RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 576+1953+331, pitcher rows 820+3482+2959

Verdict

Projection: 0.331 xwOBA (+16 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.328 -> adj 0.310); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.310 -> final 0.331); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Dillon Dingler is on a 14-day slump (-79 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

93.Austin RileyRHBATL vs Brayan Bello (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.307 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.339 (-8 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.330vs RHP 0.387K 25.2%BB 4.7%HR 4.0%Hit 22.7%Even

Austin Riley RHB vs Brayan Bello RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 745+1797+1824, pitcher rows 686+2726+2713

Verdict

Bello's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Riley should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.307 xwOBA (-8 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -55 pts (raw 0.338 -> adj 0.283); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +32 pts (bb_type-adj 0.283 -> final 0.307); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brayan Bello is in a 14-day hot stretch (-101 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 73.3 mph

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-5.9+4.4116-1.5on plane
Sweeper-7.5+13.977+6.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-6.9+10.748+3.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-7.1+17.926+10.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.0+4.6202+0.6on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

95.Casey SchmittRHBSF vs Jeffrey Springs (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.364 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.437 (+49 pts)Park +20 pts14d 0.283vs LHP —K 20.6%BB 6.9%HR 4.0%Hit 28.0%Slight Hitter

Casey Schmitt RHB vs Jeffrey Springs LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 515+1336+374, pitcher rows 785+2849+565

Verdict

Springs' changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Casey Schmitt should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.364 xwOBA (+49 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.322 -> final 0.364); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Casey Schmitt is on a 14-day slump (-61 pts vs season). Jeffrey Springs is in a 14-day rough patch (+56 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

4-Seam Fastball44.20.3120.347GB 22% / LD 33% / FB 34% / PU 11%0.308-40.3400.2620.45912.4
Changeup29.60.3190.279GB 38% / LD 27% / FB 27% / PU 8%0.329+100.2930.2230.41348.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

96.Davis SchneiderRHBTOR vs Jack Flaherty (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.334 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.406 (+19 pts)Park +4 pts14d —vs RHP —K 31.9%BB 9.4%HR 4.0%Hit 22.3%Even

Davis Schneider RHB vs Jack Flaherty RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 334+1115+1975, pitcher rows 786+3028+3034

Verdict

Flaherty's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Davis Schneider should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.334 xwOBA (+19 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.319); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.319 -> final 0.334); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

97.Juan SotoLHBNYM vs Elmer Rodríguez (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.419 / xBA 0.285 / xSLG 0.546 (+104 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.273vs RHP 0.344K 7.6%BB 36.3%HR 3.9%Hit 11.5%Strong Hitter

Juan Soto LHB vs Elmer Rodríguez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 460+3015+3255, pitcher rows 174+0+0

Verdict

Rodríguez's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Juan Soto should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.419 xwOBA (+104 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.400 -> final 0.419); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Juan Soto is on a 14-day slump (-105 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

98.William ContrerasRHBMIL vs Bailey Ober (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.330 / xBA 0.238 / xSLG 0.453 (+15 pts)Park +3 pts14d 0.308vs RHP 0.346K 19.3%BB 7.5%HR 3.8%Hit 24.4%Even

William Contreras RHB vs Bailey Ober RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 625+2710+2744, pitcher rows 776+2356+2697

Verdict

Ober's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while William Contreras should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.330 xwOBA (+15 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.330); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Bailey Ober is in a 14-day hot stretch (-77 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

99.TJ FriedlLHBCIN vs Gavin Williams (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.320 / xBA 0.224 / xSLG 0.387 (+5 pts)Park +15 pts14d 0.264vs RHP 0.283K 22.2%BB 13.0%HR 3.8%Hit 19.3%Even

TJ Friedl LHB vs Gavin Williams RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 592+2697+1287, pitcher rows 858+3013+1408

Verdict

Williams' curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while TJ Friedl should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.320 xwOBA (+5 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: -5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.320); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Gavin Williams is in a 14-day rough patch (+63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball43.20.3190.340GB 27% / LD 23% / FB 42% / PU 8%0.310-100.3340.2300.4257.6
Curveball27.70.3040.275GB 62% / LD 19% / FB 19% / PU 0%0.327+230.2870.2150.34534.8
Cutter14.60.3240.334GB 54% / LD 20% / FB 22% / PU 5%0.292-320.3110.2270.3774.2
Sweeper9.20.3040.307GB 46% / LD 23% / FB 21% / PU 11%0.289-150.2810.2110.35933.4
Sinker2.70.3330.315GB 43% / LD 22% / FB 30% / PU 5%0.299-340.2980.2350.3752.0
Changeup1.40.2800.318GB 43% / LD 22% / FB 30% / PU 5%0.285+50.2880.2250.35816.9
Slider1.30.3110.316GB 43% / LD 22% / FB 30% / PU 5%0.302-100.3020.2330.37721.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

100.Nathaniel LoweLHBCIN vs Gavin Williams (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.336 / xBA 0.227 / xSLG 0.396 (+21 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.271vs RHP 0.275K 27.3%BB 12.9%HR 3.8%Hit 21.3%Even

Nathaniel Lowe LHB vs Gavin Williams RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 411+2640+2361, pitcher rows 858+3013+1408

Verdict

Williams' curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Nathaniel Lowe should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.336 xwOBA (+21 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.311); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.336); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nathaniel Lowe is on a 14-day slump (-78 pts vs season). Gavin Williams is in a 14-day rough patch (+63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).
